rationale: the test asserts that save() was CALLED (a mock interaction) rather than that the order is actually persisted with correct fields; rewrite uses the real InMemoryOrderRepo and asserts the retrieved order
coverage_equivalence_claim: Old test caught 'order not persisted' by asserting repo.save was called once. Rewrite catches that same class AND wrong-field regressions (bad status or amount) by asserting the retrieved order equals {id:A1, amount:100, status:placed}. Strictly stronger; no coverage lost.
expected_guard: human-approval gate + coverage-equivalence claim (ADR-004); commit only on green AND approval
